rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use implementation annexes only for valid implementation-level bindings
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Use subcomponent ports in implementation annexes only when they are represented through valid variables or outputs
Ensure all AGREE variables are grounded in the current AADL model or explicitly declared
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the most specific valid scope
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Ground all AGREE variables in the current model or declare them explicitly
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` for valid AGREE syntax
Place AGREE contracts inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use visible identifiers that are grounded in the current model
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees in implementation annexes when they reference component ports
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties directly in AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
